userStory: As a guest user, I want the Home page to display guest-state controls and guest-accessible feature buttons, so that I can use the application before login and open the Login page from the Person/Profile button.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state

This workflow validates: - Initial Home page load in guest state - Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls - Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls - Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view - Navigation from guest-accessible feature buttons to their supported destinations - Absence or non-actionability of authenticated-only controls and feature buttons - Absence of the logged-in user name button while unauthenticated

Launch the application at http://localhost/washtabui/home - Verify the Home page loads in guest state - Verify the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons are visible and enabled - Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to the Login page or Login view - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to the customer-search page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to the vehicle-survey page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to the survey page - Verify authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are not visible or not actionable while in guest state
Application entry URL: http://localhost/washtabui/home - No additional approved credentials or user data were provided - Guest-state execution should remain unauthenticated throughout the workflow

Home Page Guest-State Elements: - Person/Profile button - Customer signup button - Clean survey button - Site survey button Shared Home Shell Controls: - Back button - Notification button - Home button Navigation Destinations: - Login page or Login view - Customer-search page - Vehicle-survey page - Survey page Ownership Guidance: - All guest-state Home controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - Shared Home shell controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource - The Login page must not own the Person/Profile button because navigation originates from Home

When an unauthenticated user launches the application, the Home page must load in guest state - Guest-state controls and approved guest-accessible feature buttons must be visible and enabled - Selecting the Person/Profile button must open the Login page or Login view - Guest-accessible feature buttons must navigate to their supported destinations - Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons must not be visible or must not be actionable in guest state - The logged-in user name button must not be visible while the user is unauthenticated - The Home page guest state and authenticated state are different states of the same Home page resource

behaviorRules: The workflow begins at: http://localhost/washtabui/home The Home page is the shared landing page for both unauthenticated and authenticated users.
This workflow validates only the guest-state behavior of the existing Home page before login.
The guest state is not a separate page from the authenticated Home state.
The application is launched at http://localhost/washtabui/home - The user is unauthenticated and has not signed in - The Home page loads in guest state

This workflow validates: - Initial Home page load in guest state - Visibility and interactivity of approved guest-state controls - Visibility and interactivity of shared Home shell controls - Navigation from the Person/Profile button to the Login page or Login view - Navigation from guest-accessible feature buttons to their supported destinations - Absence or non-actionability of authenticated-only controls and feature buttons - Absence of the logged-in user name button while unauthenticated

Launch the application at http://localhost/washtabui/home - Verify the Home page loads in guest state - Verify the Person/Profile button, Back button, Notification button, Home button, and approved guest-accessible feature buttons are visible and enabled - Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to the Login page or Login view - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to the customer-search page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to the vehicle-survey page - Return to the Home page guest state as needed - Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to the survey page - Verify authenticated-only controls and the logged-in user name button are not visible or not actionable while in guest state
Application entry URL: http://localhost/washtabui/home - No additional approved credentials or user data were provided - Guest-state execution should remain unauthenticated throughout the workflow

Home Page Guest-State Elements: - Person/Profile button - Customer signup button - Clean survey button - Site survey button Shared Home Shell Controls: - Back button - Notification button - Home button Navigation Destinations: - Login page or Login view - Customer-search page - Vehicle-survey page - Survey page Ownership Guidance: - All guest-state Home controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - Shared Home shell controls belong to the Home page POM/resource - The Person/Profile button belongs to the Home page POM/resource - The Login page must not own the Person/Profile button because navigation originates from Home

When an unauthenticated user launches the application, the Home page must load in guest state - Guest-state controls and approved guest-accessible feature buttons must be visible and enabled - Selecting the Person/Profile button must open the Login page or Login view - Guest-accessible feature buttons must navigate to their supported destinations - Authenticated-only controls and authenticated-only feature buttons must not be visible or must not be actionable in guest state - The logged-in user name button must not be visible while the user is unauthenticated - The Home page guest state and authenticated state are different states of the same Home page resource

Validate successful Home page load in guest state - Validate visibility and enabled state of: - Person/Profile button - Back button - Notification button - Home button - Customer signup button - Clean survey button - Site survey button - Validate navigation from: - Person/Profile button to Login page or Login view - Customer signup button to customer-search page - Clean survey button to vehicle-survey page - Site survey button to survey page - Validate authenticated-only controls and feature buttons are absent or non-actionable in guest state - Validate the logged-in user name button is not visible in guest state

Launching the application while unauthenticated transitions the application into Home page guest state - Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions from Home guest state to the Login page or Login view - Clicking Customer signup transitions from Home guest state to the customer-search page - Clicking Clean survey transitions from Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page - Clicking Site survey transitions from Home guest state to the survey page - While remaining unauthenticated, authenticated-only controls must not become accessible

Establish the Home page as the foundational reusable Home page resource/POM - Reuse the Home page resource across future guest-state and authenticated-state workflows - Reuse shared Home shell controls from the Home page resource instead of duplicating ownership in downstream resources - Reuse the Person/Profile button from the Home page resource for Login navigation workflows - Avoid assigning ownership of the Person/Profile button to the Login page resource

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user and the guest-accessible Home controls are displayed without showing authenticated user indicators.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify all approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible and enabled on Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, clickable, and available in the Home page guest state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Person/Profile button navigates from Home guest state to Login page or Login view
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while remaining unauthenticated before login submission.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Customer signup button navigates to customer-search page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Clean survey button navigates to vehicle-survey page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Site survey button navigates to survey page from Home guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state execution remains unauthenticated after navigating to Login view from Person/Profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The user remains unauthenticated throughout the navigation flow and returning to Home displays the same guest-state controls without any logged-in user indicators.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button remains enabled and accessible in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home button remains enabled and the application stays on the Home guest state without displaying an error or changing to an authenticated state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button is visible and does not expose authenticated functionality in guest state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Back button interaction does not expose authenticated-only functionality and the application remains within unauthenticated guest-state behavior.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify repeated clicks on Customer signup button do not create duplicate customer-search transitions
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions cleanly to the customer-search page only once without duplicate navigation layers, duplicate requests, or UI instability.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state controls remain visible after returning to Home page from Customer signup flow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page returns to the guest state with the Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons visible and enabled.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state controls remain visible after returning to Home page from Clean survey flow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state buttons visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest-state controls remain visible after returning to Home page from Site survey flow
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state controls visible, enabled, and still operating in unauthenticated mode.

validations: All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, clickable, and available in the Home page guest state.
The Home button remains enabled and the application stays on the Home guest state without displaying an error or changing to an authenticated state.
The Home page returns to the guest state with the Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons visible and enabled.
The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state buttons visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.
The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state controls visible, enabled, and still operating in unauthenticated mode.
transitions: The Home page loads successfully in guest state for an unauthenticated user and the guest-accessible Home controls are displayed without showing authenticated user indicators.
All approved guest-state controls and shared Home shell controls are visible, enabled, clickable, and available in the Home page guest state.
The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page or Login view while remaining unauthenticated before login submission.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the customer-search page and the user remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the vehicle-survey page and the user remains unauthenticated.
The application navigates from the Home guest state to the survey page and the user remains unauthenticated.
The user remains unauthenticated throughout the navigation flow and returning to Home displays the same guest-state controls without any logged-in user indicators.
The Home button remains enabled and the application stays on the Home guest state without displaying an error or changing to an authenticated state.
The Back button interaction does not expose authenticated-only functionality and the application remains within unauthenticated guest-state behavior.
The Home page returns to the guest state with the Person/Profile, Back, Notification, Home, Customer signup, Clean survey, and Site survey buttons visible and enabled.
The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state buttons visible and enabled and without authenticated user indicators.
The Home page returns to the guest state with all approved guest-state controls visible, enabled, and still operating in unauthenticated mode.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
